name: SOL Value Reacceptance Exhaustion v1
thesis: In transitional SOL 4h conditions, exhaustion moves can reverse only after price reaccepts prior value. The edge should come from broad reclaim-after-stretch behavior rather than raw oversold or overbought touches.
edge_mechanism: exhaustion mean reversion after value reacceptance
invalidation_condition: Abort if this remains too sparse, internally contradictory, or cannot maintain post-cost edge in blind windows.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Overextended moves that snap back only once value is reaccepted rather than continuing trendlessly.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Exhaustion alone is noisy, but exhaustion plus value reacceptance can catch positioning unwind with cleaner confirmation.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: SOL 4h frequently overshoots local value and then mean reverts hard enough to support structured exits.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the asset trends persistently instead of snapping back, stretch-based reacceptance entries may underfire or fail quickly.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If some trades appear but density is weak, widen the acceptable reacceptance band before touching exits.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; stretch and reclaim are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by proximity-to-band logic instead of exact touches only

long: Take profit: EMA_20 + 1.4 * ATR_14 or 1.6 * ATR_14, whichever comes first
short: Take profit: EMA_20 - 1.4 * ATR_14 or 1.6 * ATR_14, whichever comes first
